items.title: Ex-soldier, comedian and aspiring Martian is the Aussie face of Laos cave rescue

children.content: Josh Richards has made news for his diving and his interplanetary ambitions. Now he's using his skills to try to rescue others.

gallery.altText: A cave diver wearing a yellow helmet, blue mask and black wetsuit squeezes between rocks.

thumbnail.altText: A cave diver wearing a yellow helmet, blue mask and black wetsuit squeezes between rocks.

items.title: Farmers flock to buy CCTV cameras to stop fuel theft

children.content: Fuel theft is prompting farmers to install CCTV cameras at unprecedented rates, leading one security company to overhaul its entire business model.

image.altText: A man in hi-vis and a cap stands in front of a diesel tank.

items.title: 'Panic started to set in': Woman rescued from quicksand on Adelaide beach

children.content: A relaxing sunset walk at the beach with a friend turned to panic for a young woman after her leg got stuck in quicksand, prompting a rescue from police and fire crews.

image.altText: A person wearing jeans shorts, their calf submerged in wet sand

items.title: Tough times for hospitality businesses with rising cost of food and power

children.content: The hospitality sector is grappling with "unprecedented" cost increases, according to a national peak body, with many being forced to close their doors.

gallery.altText: A woman leans an arm on a food counter in a restaurant

thumbnail.altText: A woman leans an arm on a food counter in a restaurant

items.title: Police scour mine shafts, drain dam in search for missing outback woman

children.content: A renewed search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf will include searching about 1,000 mine shafts and draining Blue Dam.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and tattoos on her arm wearing a black singlet smiles while sitting at a bar

items.title: Public servant to take SA government to court over golf course redevelopment

children.content: An Adelaide man alleges the state government failed to properly refer the controversial North Adelaide Golf Course to the federal environment minister for approval.

image.altText: A man wearing a green shirt standing in front of a line of trees and a dirt path while holding folders

items.title: Paddle-steamer first launched 120 years ago returns to working life

children.content: After being raised from the bottom of the river, dug out of the mud, and lovingly restored, the PS Canally is ready to take passengers up and down the River Murray.

image.altText: The PS Canally is moored on a river. It is white and grey with colourful flags hung up on it.

items.title: Adelaide Hills man pleads guilty to murdering mother

children.content: A man who previously denied murdering his mother at their home in 2022, and even gave evidence in his own defence, has a last-minute change of heart and pleads guilty to murder.

image.altText: A bald man with a beard wearing handcuffs and a plaid shirt

items.title: South Australia braces for severe weather and damaging winds

children.content: The State Emergency Service has advised people to move vehicles away from trees and secure loose items around properties as South Australia braces for damaging winds.

image.altText: Two women cross the street in the rain with one protected under an umbrella and the other pulling it towards her.

items.title: Roadside stalls are booming, with 45,000 members in one group alone

children.content: Nestled away in the Adelaide Hills are dozens of roadside stalls dedicated to local produce, including baked goods and bouquets, and business is booming.

image.altText: A woman with stands next to a wooden stall stocked with loaves of bread. She wears jeans and a tshirt, smiling

items.title: 'Bit of a gold rush' as companies search for underground hydrogen

children.content: It's been about for a century, but companies are springing to action to find "gold hydrogen" underground around the country, as Australia and the world strive to reach net zero emissions by 2050.

image.altText: A drill rig at night with trucks and lights surrounding it.

items.title: Australia could still buy five US submarines if needed, defence minister says

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended a shake-up to AUKUS that would involve Australia buying three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the United States.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks during an AUKUS Defence ministers' press huddle

items.title: After nearly 80 years, the actors in this film have finally been understood

children.content: The "huge swathes" of Pitjantjatjara lines in the 1949 film Bitter Springs are at last translated.
Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

image.altText: A film poster with the words Bitter Springs plastered onto it, depicting Aboriginal men with traditional weapons

items.title: Tax deduction red flags on the ATO's radar

children.content: The Australian Taxation Office (ATO) is warning Australians to stop claiming private expenses on their tax returns. The agency gives its tips this tax time, including the dos and don'ts of claiming work-related expenses and investment losses.

image.altText: A tax return form with red flag pins in it

items.title: Real estate agency using fake AI salesperson to spam customers

children.content: "Daniel" is an AI real estate agent who calls strangers and secretly records conversations without their consent.

image.altText: A stern woman holds a phone

items.title: The impact of Australia only buying second-hand submarines under AUKUS

children.content: Australia will only buy second-hand Virginia nuclear-powered submarines from the US during the AUKUS stopgap phase, with some analysts criticising the move and others believing it is a positive change of plan.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

items.title: Workmate of missing Jack McLennan says he's 'given police absolutely everything'

children.content: The last known person to see missing Queensland man Jack McLennan alive has broken his public silence amid intense community speculation over his role in Mr McLennan's disappearance.

gallery.altText: Chris Duke wearing a hi-vis yellow shirt and sunglasses

thumbnail.altText: Chris Duke wearing a hi-vis yellow shirt and sunglasses

items.title: Record number of Australian billionaires hold total wealth of $686b

children.content: Australia now has 178 billionaires who collectively hold more than $686 billion in wealth, sparking debate about the advantages and disadvantages of the growing number of ultra-wealthy.

image.altText: workers commuting

items.title: Parent 'thought it was a joke' upon hearing year 3 NAPLAN tests stolen

children.content: Dozens of primary school students are victims of an analogue data breach after their NAPLAN tests were stolen from a van in Melbourne's inner-north on April Fool's Day.

image.altText: two school girls write in a book during class.

items.title: Neighbours complain about 200 chopper flights a week into luxury resort

children.content: A luxury resort that has been shut for three years hopes to reopen by chauffeuring guests down a steep 4WD track and flying them in on more than 200 helicopter flights a week.

gallery.altText: A building with the lights on, on a green slope next to a mountain shrouded in clouds.

thumbnail.altText: A building with the lights on, on a green slope next to a mountain shrouded in clouds.

items.title: UK's ex-US envoy slams 'bereft' Starmer leadership in new document drop

children.content: Among the more than 1,500 pages of documents were messages from Peter Mandelson voicing strong criticisms of Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer's leadership of the UK government.

image.altText: A close up of Keir Starmer's emotionless ace, while wearing dark-rimmed glasses and a blue shirt.

items.title: 'Happy [and safe] shooting!' AI chatbot's chilling message

children.content: An investigation into how AI chatbots respond to violent requests produces "deeply disturbing" results, as OpenAI denies ChatGPT encourages crime.

image.altText: A close up of a finger pointing to a DeepSeek app icon on a smartphone

items.title: Growing up in a cult partly inspired performer's latest show

children.content: Carly Sheppard is perhaps best known for experimental performance, but a coincidental meeting with a fellow dancer convinced her to go back to her roots.

gallery.altText: Alisdair Macindoe and Carly Sheppard, both early 40s, stand in a field, among farming equipment..

thumbnail.altText: Alisdair Macindoe and Carly Sheppard, both early 40s, stand in a field, among farming equipment..

items.title: Quiz: Are you smart enough to understand the Scripps Spelling Bee?

children.content: We don't think you'll have even heard of most of these words, let alone know how to spell them.

image.altText: A teenage boy holds a trophy on his head, next to an event official

items.title: Why make-up sex may not be fixing anything

children.content: Experts say if a fight keeps ending up in the bedroom, it could be a sign you and your partner aren't resolving anything.

image.altText: A couples legs lying in bed

items.title: Antibiotic found in wild seafood 14 kilometres from salmon farm

children.content: Tasmania's Natural Resources and Environment Department has confirmed the antibiotic florfenicol was detected at a 'low level' in wild seafood 14 kilometres from where the drug was administered.

gallery.altText: Tassal salmon pens in Macquarie Harbour, southwest Tasmania.

thumbnail.altText: Tassal salmon pens in Macquarie Harbour, southwest Tasmania.

items.title: Regional voters consider their options as One Nation popularity surges

children.content: Pauline Hanson's right-wing populist party is leading opinion polls for the first time, and regional Queenslanders say they can see why.

image.altText: Man with blue eyes and dark hair looks off centre

items.title: Chalmers accepts voters 'legitimate concerns' as One Nation overtakes Labor

children.content: Labor's federal budget is falling flat with its target demographic as millennials say they would rather vote for One Nation than Labor or the Greens, according to a new Redbridge poll.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

items.title: Cats eradicated from Little Dog Island

children.content: Penguins have been sighted on a remote Bass Strait island for the first time in decades after the successful eradication of feral cats and possums.

gallery.altText: A detection dog near the beach at Little Dog Island

thumbnail.altText: A detection dog near the beach at Little Dog Island
Short tailed shearwaters Little Dog Island - Sue Robinson

items.title: Julie's life was in disarray. Then she found a safe haven in Sydney

children.content: A centuries-old mansion in Sydney became Julie's safe haven, following a series of difficult life circumstances. She is one of many women being assisted through meanwhile use housing.

image.altText: A meeting about women's housing shelter

items.title: Miley Cyrus emails and emoji texts amid Melbourne film festival data leak

children.content: Melbourne International Film Festival customers receive strange texts and emails after a data breach linked to the event's third-party ticketing system.

image.altText: A crowd of people for a Melbourne International Film Festival event.

items.title: 'Deeply concerning outbreak': Australia keeps borders open as Ebola spreads

children.content: The federal government will not impose border restrictions on travellers from Ebola-stricken countries despite new suspected cases in Italy and Brazil, as the worsening outbreak prompts other countries to take action.

gallery.altText: A group of workers in head-to-toe medical protective gear walk in a line as the lead worker sprays a substance on the ground.

thumbnail.altText: A group of workers in head-to-toe medical protective gear walk in a line as the lead worker sprays a substance on the ground.
